title: Berlin Audio Guide App vs No-App - Quick Summary
items: **Use an audio guide app when the platform is the point.** A large catalogue, GPS triggers, integrated maps and several cities in one account can justify the install, login and storage.
**Use a no-app browser route for one focused Berlin walk.** It opens on the phone you already have and removes the app-store, account and notification layer before the first chapter.
**Save the MP3 and route card on Wi-Fi.** The browser is the convenient front door; the downloaded files are the fallback when signal becomes weak outside a station or memorial site.

**Offline maps do not solve every walking problem.** Google notes that walking and public-transport directions are unavailable offline, so keep named stops and route-card directions as well as the map.
**All five BerlinWalk paid routes cost €4.99.** Hidden Berlin, Berlin Wall, Medieval Berlin, Third Reich Berlin and Kreuzberg Street Art use the same browser-plus-backup format.
**My recommendation:** use an app for a multi-city catalogue. For one Berlin route this afternoon, open the browser route, download the backup and put the phone away between chapters.
